text:
Former Michigan swimmer Siobhán Haughey was back on the podium Wednesday.

The Hong Kong native earned her second medal of the Paris Olympics by earning bronze in the women’s 100-meter freestyle inside Paris La Defense Arena. She clocked the top qualifying time at 52.64 seconds before posting a time of 52.33 seconds in the final, finishing only behind Sweden’s Sarah Sjoestroem (52.16) and American Torri Huske (52.29).

Two races, TWO bronzes‼️🥉What a performance by @siobhanhaughey01#OlympiansMadeHere#MGoParispic.twitter.com/EANsjKZMWK

Haughey, a 15-time Big Ten champion at Michigan from 2015-19, narrowly edged Australia’s Mollie O’Callaghan (52.34) for her fourth overall Olympic medal. She alsotook bronze in the 200 freestyle on Mondayand won silver in the 100 and 200 at the 2020 Games in Tokyo.

Paris is her third Olympic Games, also competing in Rio in 2016 before her sophomore year for the Wolverines. Haughey is the only Wolverines women’s swimmer to win two individual medals in the same Olympic games.

The 26-year-old is one of four current or former Wolverines to receive a medal in Paris, along with Fred Richard (bronze, men’s gymnastics), Paul Juda (bronze, men’s gymnastics) and Alena Olsen (women’s rugby).

Ryan Zuke is a sports reporter for MLive.com and covers University of Michigan athletics. He writes about a variety of sports with a primary focus on football and recruiting. With the success of many Wolverines...more
